Microbenchmarking NVIDIA’s Blackwell Architecture: An in-depth ...
The Evolution of Nvidia Blackwell GPU Memory Architecture
NVIDIA introduces Blackwell GPU lineup - CUDO Compute

findings: The GB200 Grace Blackwell Superchip pairs one Grace CPU with two Blackwell GPUs via NVLink Chip-to-Chip (NVLink-C2C), creating a unified coherent memory architecture called Unified CPU-GPU Memory or Extended GPU Memory (EGM).

The Blackwell B200 GPU uses a dual-die design where each die contains 96 GB of HBM3e memory, totaling 192 GB (180 GB usable after ECC/system overhead); the two dies are connected by NVIDIA's NV-HBI at 10 TB/s.

The Blackwell B200 GPU achieves 8 TB/s of HBM3e memory bandwidth, a 2.4x improvement over the Hopper H100's 3.35 TB/s HBM3 bandwidth, which is critical for keeping tensor cores fed during large matrix multiplications.

The Blackwell B200 GPU's L2 cache was increased to approximately 126 MB total (~64–65 MB per die), a 2.5x increase over Hopper's 50 MB, reducing trips to HBM by keeping more neural network weights and intermediate results on-chip.

The Blackwell B200 GPU includes eight HBM3e memory stacks in an 8-Hi stack design (eight DRAM dies stacked vertically, each providing 3 GB), with four stacks per die, for 192 GB total on-package memory.
